new_classes.definition_en: A non-alcoholic beverage classified under CN 2202 99 19 that constitutes a tonic preparation, often marketed as a food supplement, which may be based on extracts from plants (including herbs) and may contain added vitamins and/or minerals.
A material substance derived by extraction from plant material, including herbs, used as an ingredient or base in food, beverage, or supplement preparations.
A material substance comprising one or more vitamins and/or minerals intentionally added to a food or beverage preparation.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the core distinguishing criteria for CN 2202 99 19: (1) non-alcoholic nature (modelled via subClassOf NonAlcoholicBeverage and the low-ABV decimalRange restriction), (2) tonic/supplement character, (3) plant/herbal extract base (via BFO has_part someValuesFrom PlantExtractIngredient), and (4) optional vitamin/mineral additives (via BFO has_part someValuesFrom VitaminOrMineralAdditive). The complement restriction against SweetenedOrFlavouredWaterBeverage is a partial proxy for sibling exclusion but does not fully formalise all siblings (e.g. exclusion from juice-based or milk-based beverages under 2202 99). The legal note also references an explanatory note to the chapter (general, second paragraph) that defines "tonic preparations" more precisely — this definition is not available in full and therefore cannot be completely axiomatised. The "often referred to as food supplements" characterisation and the open-ended ingredient list ("etc.") mean that coverage is necessarily partial. The hasIngredient property was introduced but the plant-extract and vitamin/mineral part restrictions use the more principled BFO has_part relation instead.
